title: VenueOps - Event Operations Platform
subtitle: ASP.NET Core 8, React, and PostgreSQL platform for venue bookings, staff assignments, guest counts, shift notes, and role-based dashboards.
summary: Production-style event operations application inspired by real hospitality workflows, with authentication, staffing, booking management, demo data, Docker setup, CI, and Render deployment.
description: VenueOps is a full-stack event operations platform built to show how software can support real venue and hospitality workflows. The application covers venue bookings, client and room management, staff assignments, guest counts, shift notes, role-aware dashboards, demo accounts, public documentation, Docker Compose local setup, and a live Render deployment.
highlights: Designed a relational data model for users, clients, venue rooms, event bookings, staff assignments, and shift notes.
Implemented JWT authentication with BCrypt password hashing and role-based access for Admin, Manager, Staff, and Demo users.
Built responsive React workflows for booking CRUD, staffing, status updates, dashboard metrics, and shift note capture.
Added automated backend and frontend tests plus GitHub Actions CI for build, test, and Docker image validation.
Published a live demo with seeded demo data, Docker Compose local setup, architecture documentation, and public project assets.
note: The public demo uses seeded demo data on Render. The project documentation describes local Docker Compose as the strongest guaranteed demo path.

title: StockPilot - Smart Inventory Management System
subtitle: ASP.NET Core MVC inventory management platform with PostgreSQL, role-based access, stock tracking, dashboard analytics, and Render deployment.
summary: Full-stack inventory management web application for small business operations with products, categories, suppliers, stock tracking, orders, user roles, and dashboard metrics.
description: StockPilot was built as a full-stack inventory management system focused on practical business workflows. The application supports product and supplier management, dashboard analytics, low-stock monitoring, stock movement history, order workflows, role-based access, and deployed demo access backed by PostgreSQL.
highlights: Built role-based authentication and authorization with ASP.NET Core Identity for Admin, Manager, and Staff workflows.
Designed PostgreSQL data models and EF Core relationships for products, categories, suppliers, orders, order items, stock movements, and audit logs.
Implemented product search, category and supplier filters, stock status filters, sorting, and pagination.
Added dashboard metrics for inventory value, potential revenue, low-stock items, out-of-stock items, recent movements, and recent orders.
Implemented stock movement tracking so inventory changes are recorded instead of only updating final quantity.
Deployed the application on Render with PostgreSQL and documented the project with screenshots, demo accounts, and setup steps.
note: The Render PostgreSQL demo database is on a free plan and may expire or reset over time.

title: Hair Stylist Booking Web Application
subtitle: Booking, portfolio, testimonial, admin, and mobile-ready product direction for a Toronto hairstylist business.
summary: Team-built booking platform for a hairstylist business with client scheduling, digital portfolio support, admin workflows, and matching web and mobile product planning.
description: This project was created to improve how a hairstylist manages appointments, availability, and digital presence. The product direction combines client booking, account-based scheduling, admin workflow support, portfolio and testimonial presentation, and a matching mobile experience for the broader service flow.
highlights: Planned and implemented a booking system focused on available time slots, rescheduling, and cancellation support.
Included admin workflow support for schedule management, appointment updates, and customer records.
Designed the product around both customer booking needs and a hairstylist workflow that reduces manual coordination.
Covered portfolio and testimonial presentation so the product also supports digital presence beyond scheduling.
Extended the product direction across web, backend API, and mobile prototype work.
note: This project is shown as portfolio case study material rather than a public demo.
